metadata.title:
WATER HIGH AND SWIFT AND
LOGS IN RIVER MAY SWEEP
OUT ALL OTHER CROSSINGS
IF THEY BREAK LOOSE
FROM BOOMS.

pages.raw_text:
1t seems to be a foregone con-
clusion now that the piers for the
Washington and Howard St.
bridges will not be built for an
other year. Since the delay
caused by the inactivity of the city
council, which did not pass the or
dinance authorizing this work I'M
til Feb. 26, the water has risen at
an alarming rate until now there
Is a depth of IA feet at Washington
st. and the current has reached 80
feet per second. Should the work
start now, the falseworll would
contract the current to such a de-
gree that it would be almost a
physical impossibility to withstand
it. This would greatly endanger
the lives of workmen, as well as
make imminent the destruction OF
the entire bridge.


Besides the danger from the cur
rent at that point, the river Is
now full of a boom of logs at the
McGoldrick plant. Everything pos.
sible is being done to fasten and
hold the boom until it can be
handled by the mill. Should the
current get much swifter this
boom may give away and the prob
abilities in that case are that ev-
cry bridge between there and the
falls would be smashed.


1t is understood that the Coates
Wallace Engineering Co., of Chi
cago, which the city council has
authorized to supply an engineer
to supervise the work by day la
Dor, will send an expert post haste
to report on conditions here. Mr..
Biers, one of their experts, is now
engaged at Portland, Ore, and in
order to save time he will prob
ably be order to Spokane at once.


j. b. Straus, the expert who re-
ported on the bridges here some
time ago, and who promised to
forward report when he got back
to Chicago, has as yet turned In no
report, and Commissioner w. T.
Roy, who is at the head of the
bridge department, is unable to ges
communication from him.


His verbal report while here
was that practically every bridge
in the city was frail, worn out, or
defective, the least defective of all
being the Monroe St bridge, which
has all along been considered the
weakest structure in the city,


1t has been minated that Mr..
Sirius was instructed to hold up the
report. as 1t was of such disturb
ing nature that the general public
would be afraid to cross any of the
bridges.
